2605.00342.title: Making Every Verified Token Count: Adaptive Verification for MoE Speculative Decoding
2605.00342.paper_summary: Tree-based speculative decoding accelerates autoregressive generation by verifying multiple draft candidates in parallel, but this advantage weakens for sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models. As the draft tree grows, different branches activate different experts, expanding the union of activated experts and substantially increasing target-side verification cost. We propose EVICT, a training-free, hyperparameter-free, and lossless adaptive verification method for MoE speculative decoding. EVICT makes every verified token count by truncating the draft tree before target verification and retaining only the cost-effective prefix. It leverages fine-grained drafter signals to estimate candidate benefit, combines them with offline-profiled verification cost, and remains highly compatible with the high-performance graph-based serving framework SGLang. Extensive experiments on diverse MoE backbones and benchmarks show that EVICT achieves up to 2.35x speedup over autoregressive decoding and an average 1.21x speedup over the state-of-the-art baseline EAGLE-3, while significantly reducing unnecessary expert activations during verification.
2605.00342.paper_summary_zh: 樹狀的推測解碼透過並行驗證多個草稿候選者來加速自回歸生成，但這一優勢在稀疏的專家混合模型（MoE）中減弱。隨著草稿樹的增長，不同的分支激活不同的專家，擴大了激活專家的聯集，並大幅增加了目標端的驗證成本。我們提出了EVICT，一種無需訓練、無需超參數且無損的自適應驗證方法，用於MoE推測解碼。EVICT通過在目標驗證之前截斷草稿樹並僅保留成本效益高的前綴，使每個已驗證的標記都能發揮作用。它利用細粒度的草擬者信號來估算候選者的效益，並將其與離線配置的驗證成本相結合，並且與高性能的基於圖的服務框架SGLang高度兼容。在多樣的MoE骨幹和基準上的廣泛實驗顯示，EVICT在自回歸解碼上實現了高達2.35倍的加速，並在最先進的基準EAGLE-3上平均實現了1.21倍的加速，同時在驗證過程中顯著減少了不必要的專家激活。
2605.00342.author: Lehan Pan et.al.
2605.00342.authors: Lehan Pan, Ziyang Tao, Ruoyu Pang, Xiao Wang, Jianjun Zhao, Yanyong Zhang
